mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive RSAFS and flat CPIAUCSL periods yield negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving factory utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during post-shock recovery periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth correlates negatively with 60-day SPX returns across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined PAYEMS and GDPC1 growth yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during liquidity crunch periods signal immediate corporate revenue shortfalls, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during stress periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 40 percent.
